masthead.dek: Every answer is something you can walk in your head: a country, a capital, a big city, a river or peak, a century, a count. Each stem hides the obvious answer behind a slicer. Narrow the field to a few finalists, place your bet, then tap to check.
masthead.footer_html: Twenty reasoning-driven questions across Europe's geography, history, languages, the EU and euro, culture, and economy — each fact verified against a cited source.

plan.note: Answer-routing: answers are drawn from bounded, walkable sets — the ~44 European countries, ~44 capitals, big cities, major rivers/lakes/peaks, centuries, and small guessable counts. Slicers remove the warhorse answer (Russia, Vatican, Volga, Mont Blanc as default) so difficulty is reasoning, not obscurity.

questions.q: Russia is by far the biggest country touching Europe, but most of it sits in Asia. Which country that lies entirely within Europe is the largest by land area?
questions.detail: Ukraine covers about 603,500 square kilometers, every bit of it in Europe.
questions.why: Once you set Russia aside, the only contenders are the big western and eastern states — and Ukraine edges out France as the largest country wholly inside Europe.
questions.more: France is next at about 551,000 sq km (metropolitan area), then Spain.
questions.source: Worldometer / World Atlas — largest countries in Europe; Ukraine ~603,500 km^2 is the largest lying entirely in Europe.
questions._mechanism: slicer (removes Russia, which is mostly in Asia) — difficulty med

questions.q: Most large EU countries use the euro. Which is the most populous EU country that still has its own national currency instead?
questions.detail: Poland has about 37 million people and still uses the zloty, not the euro.
questions.why: Germany, France, Italy and Spain all use the euro, so the heuristic is 'biggest EU country that opted out' — and that's Poland, the fifth-largest member overall.
questions.more: Among non-euro EU members, Sweden, Czechia, Hungary and Romania all trail Poland in population.
questions.source: Worldometer EU populations 2026; European Commission euro-area membership (Poland uses the zloty).
questions._mechanism: slicer (removes the big euro users Germany/France/Italy/Spain) — difficulty med

questions.q: Which European river flows through or borders more countries than any other river in the world?
questions.detail: The Danube runs through or along the edge of 10 countries on its way to the Black Sea.
questions.why: Length points you toward the Volga, but the Volga sits inside one country — the question is about countries touched, and the Danube's east-flowing path across central Europe touches the most of any river anywhere.
questions.more: Its ten: Germany, Austria, Slovakia, Hungary, Croatia, Serbia, Romania, Bulgaria, Moldova and Ukraine.
questions.source: Britannica, Danube River — flows through or borders 10 countries, the most of any river in the world.
questions._mechanism: reflex-defeater (vs Volga, the longest) — difficulty med

questions.q: Counting only people in the European Union, which language has the most native speakers?
questions.detail: German is the first language of roughly 90 to 95 million people inside the EU.
questions.why: Russian wins across the wider continent, but Russia isn't in the EU — so the answer narrows to the bloc's big-population states, and German edges out French and Italian.
questions.more: It's spoken natively not just in Germany but in Austria, Luxembourg, and parts of Belgium and Italy.
questions.source: Languages of the European Union (Wikipedia) — German is the most widely spoken native language in the EU (~90M+).
questions._mechanism: slicer (removes Russian by restricting to the EU) — difficulty med

questions.q: Which European country has four official languages at the national level?
questions.detail: Switzerland recognizes German, French, Italian, and Romansh.
questions.why: Most countries lean on one or two tongues; the standout with four is the Alpine federation wedged between German-, French-, and Italian-speaking neighbors.
questions.more: Romansh, spoken by under 1% of Swiss, is the smallest of the four and is only semi-official nationwide.
questions.source: Swiss Federal Constitution, Art. 4 — national languages are German, French, Italian, and Romansh.
questions._mechanism: identity / count — difficulty med

questions.q: Among capital cities on the European mainland, which one sits farthest north?
questions.detail: Helsinki sits at about 60.2 degrees north, the northernmost mainland capital in Europe.
questions.why: Reykjavik is farther north but sits on an island, so on the mainland the answer is the northernmost of the Nordic capitals — and Helsinki edges out Oslo, Stockholm and Tallinn.
questions.more: Oslo follows at about 59.9 degrees north, then Tallinn and Stockholm.
questions.source: World Atlas, northernmost capitals; Helsinki ~60.17 N is the northernmost mainland-Europe capital.
questions._mechanism: slicer (removes island capital Reykjavik) — difficulty hard

questions.q: Which country was the most recent to swap out its old currency and start using the euro?
questions.detail: Croatia joined the euro on 1 January 2023, becoming the 20th member of the euro area.
questions.why: The euro hasn't expanded often lately, so 'the newest member' is a single recent event — Croatia retired the kuna at the start of 2023.
questions.more: Before Croatia, the most recent addition was Lithuania back in 2015.
questions.source: Council of the EU / ECB — Croatia adopted the euro on 1 January 2023 as the 20th euro-area member.
questions._mechanism: non-decorative route (recency, year not stated in stem) — difficulty hard

questions.q: Which European country shares a land border with nine others, the most of any country on the continent?
questions.detail: Germany borders nine countries: Denmark, Poland, Czechia, Austria, Switzerland, France, Luxembourg, Belgium, and the Netherlands.
questions.why: A country smack in the middle of the continent racks up neighbors fast — and the central, widest-spanning state touches nine, more than France or Russia's European stretch.
questions.more: France and Austria tie for second among European countries, with eight neighbors each.
questions.source: deutschland.de — Germany shares borders with nine countries, the most on the continent.
questions._mechanism: max — difficulty med

questions.q: Set aside tiny non-members like the Vatican and Monaco. Which actual member of the European Union is the smallest by land area?
questions.detail: Malta covers about 316 square kilometers, smaller than any other EU country.
questions.why: The Vatican and Monaco are the reflex 'smallest in Europe' answers but aren't in the EU — so among members it's the Mediterranean island nation, smaller even than Luxembourg.
questions.more: Luxembourg, the next-smallest EU state, is more than eight times Malta's size.
questions.source: Member states of the EU (Wikipedia); Malta ~316 km^2 is the smallest EU member by area.
questions._mechanism: slicer (removes non-EU microstates Vatican/Monaco) — difficulty med

questions.q: Which mountain is the highest in Greece, and was said in old stories to be the home of the gods?
questions.detail: Its highest summit, Mytikas, reaches 2,918 meters, the tallest point in Greece.
questions.why: Greece's tallest peak is also its most famous one — the same mountain ancient myth named as the seat of the gods.
questions.more: Mytikas was first climbed in modern times only in 1913.
questions.source: Britannica / Wikipedia, Mount Olympus — Mytikas 2,917.7 m is the highest peak in Greece.
questions._mechanism: max (mythic cue routes without naming height) — difficulty med

questions.q: Six countries signed the 1951 treaty that started the project that grew into today's European Union. Three were France, Italy, and West Germany. Which small country, now a major EU capital host, was a fourth founder along with Belgium and the Netherlands?
questions.detail: Luxembourg was one of the six founders of the European Coal and Steel Community in 1951.
questions.why: The founding six were France, West Germany, Italy and the three Benelux states — given Belgium and the Netherlands, the missing Benelux member is the small Grand Duchy.
questions.more: Luxembourg City today hosts the EU's Court of Justice and other institutions.
questions.source: Treaty of Paris (1951) — founders: Belgium, France, Italy, Luxembourg, the Netherlands, West Germany.
questions._mechanism: non-decorative route (Benelux deduction) — difficulty med

questions.q: Which lake lying wholly within Europe has the largest surface area?
questions.detail: Lake Ladoga, near St. Petersburg, covers about 17,700 square kilometers.
questions.why: The Caspian is bigger but straddles into Asia; restricted to lakes fully inside Europe, the winner is the large freshwater lake in Russia's northwest.
questions.more: Lake Onega, also in northwest Russia, is the runner-up at about 9,700 sq km.
questions.source: Britannica, Lake Ladoga — ~17,700 km^2, the largest lake located entirely in Europe.
questions._mechanism: slicer (removes the part-Asian Caspian) — difficulty hard

questions.q: Europe's biggest lakes mostly sit in Russia, which isn't in the EU. Which is the largest lake that lies inside the European Union?
questions.detail: Lake Vanern in Sweden covers about 5,650 square kilometers, the largest in the EU.
questions.why: Ladoga and Onega are bigger but Russian, so within the bloc the answer shifts to Scandinavia, where Sweden's largest lake takes the title.
questions.more: It's the third-largest lake in Europe overall, after the two Russian giants.
questions.source: List of largest lakes of Europe (Wikipedia) — Vanern ~5,650 km^2 is the largest lake in the EU.
questions._mechanism: slicer (removes non-EU Russian lakes) — difficulty hard

questions.q: Germany has the largest economy in the European Union. Which EU country has the second-largest?
questions.detail: France has the EU's second-biggest economy, with GDP on the order of 3 trillion euros.
questions.why: Once Germany is removed, the next tier is the other big western economies — and France sits just ahead of Italy and Spain inside the EU.
questions.more: The UK's economy is larger than France's but left the EU in 2020.
questions.source: StatisticsTimes / Visual Capitalist — France is the second-largest EU economy after Germany.
questions._mechanism: slicer (removes Germany, the max) — difficulty med

questions.q: Thanks to thousands of inlets and islands, which European country has by far the longest coastline, even though it isn't especially large?
questions.detail: Norway's mainland-and-island coastline stretches well over 80,000 kilometers.
questions.why: Coastline length is about ruggedness, not size — and the country whose whole edge is fjords and skerries dwarfs everyone else.
questions.more: Measured with all its islands, the figure climbs past 100,000 km depending on method.
questions.source: Coastline of Norway (Wikipedia) — ~83,000 km with islands, the longest in Europe (excluding Russia).
questions._mechanism: non-decorative route (fjord cue, not area) — difficulty med

questions.q: Which EU member is an island nation in the eastern Mediterranean, far from the rest of the bloc and split by a long-running dispute?
questions.detail: Cyprus, an island near the Levant, joined the EU in 2004; its northern third is under separate Turkish-Cypriot control.
questions.why: An eastern-Mediterranean island that's in the EU narrows fast — Malta is central Mediterranean, so the divided eastern island is the answer.
questions.more: It's the EU's easternmost member, lying farther east than any mainland member capital.
questions.source: Outline of Cyprus (Wikipedia) — EU member since 2004; island in the eastern Mediterranean, divided since 1974.
questions._mechanism: identity (slicer removes Malta via 'eastern') — difficulty med

questions.q: England and France fought a famous on-and-off conflict named for about how long it ran. To the nearest hundred years, how long did it actually last?
questions.detail: The Hundred Years' War ran from 1337 to 1453 — 116 years of intermittent fighting.
questions.why: The name says a hundred, and the real span sits just past it; rounded to the nearest hundred you still land near 100, but the precise figure is 116.
questions.more: It ended with the French victory at Castillon in 1453, which pushed England off the continent.
questions.source: Wikipedia / World History Encyclopedia — Hundred Years' War, 1337-1453, lasting 116 years.
questions._mechanism: count (name is a reflex-defeater vs the true 116) — difficulty med

questions.q: Which European country holds more UNESCO World Heritage Sites than any other?
questions.detail: Italy has 61 World Heritage Sites, the most of any country in the world.
questions.why: A country layered with Roman ruins, Renaissance cities and historic landscapes tops the list — Italy leads not just Europe but the whole world.
questions.more: Germany and France follow in Europe, with 55 and 54 sites.
questions.source: Statista / UNESCO — Italy leads with 61 World Heritage Sites (as of 2025).
questions._mechanism: max — difficulty med

questions.q: Mount Elbrus in the Caucasus is Europe's highest point, but many leave it off as too far east. Which peak is the highest in the Alps and in western Europe?
questions.detail: Mont Blanc, on the France-Italy border, rises to about 4,808 meters.
questions.why: Set the Caucasus aside and the tallest peak shifts to the high Alps — and the single highest summit there straddles the French-Italian frontier.
questions.more: Its exact height shifts a little each year with the snow cap on the summit.
questions.source: Wikipedia / Britannica, Mont Blanc — ~4,808 m, highest peak in the Alps and western Europe.
questions._mechanism: slicer (removes Elbrus by restricting to the Alps/western Europe) — difficulty med

questions.q: Among capital cities on the European mainland, which one sits farthest south?
questions.detail: Athens, at about 38 degrees north, is the southernmost capital on the European mainland.
questions.why: Malta's Valletta is farther south but sits on an island, so on the mainland the answer is the capital of the country reaching deepest into the Mediterranean — Greece.
questions.more: Lisbon and Madrid are the next-southernmost mainland capitals, both a couple degrees higher.
questions.source: StudyCountry / World Atlas — Athens (~37.98 N) is the southernmost capital on the European mainland.
questions._mechanism: slicer (removes island capital Valletta) — difficulty hard
